commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1964a6533f78baad1b8e8d38d1a11664040b95f9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds app-level duplicate launch handling, Launch at Login status-aware registration, keychain prompt serialization, cold-start validation scripts, package/run script hardening, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the duplicate guard ignores bundleURL and the prepare wrapper imports PIL even though the validator uses stdlib_png. I did not run the macOS runtime path because this is a read-only review and that validation can trigger app/keychain/UI behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR lacks contributor real behavior proof and the duplicate-launch policy needs maintainer approval or revision before merge.

target.title: Add cold-start validation follow-ups
target.body:
## Summary

This PR splits the remaining cold-start follow-up work out of #1150 after #1231 landed the low-risk menu-readiness subset.

It focuses on the launch, login-item, keychain-prompt, package/run, and validation-harness pieces that were intentionally left out of #1231 for separate review and proof.

## Relationship to earlier PRs

- #1150 proposed the broader cold-start UI readiness effort, including menu refreshes, duplicate-launch protection, login-item hardening, keychain prompt serialization, packaging checks, and a reboot validation harness.
- #1231 extracted and merged only the low-risk menu-readiness/cache-hydration subset from #1150.
- This PR carries the remaining scoped follow-ups from #1150 that were not merged in #1231, without reintroducing the already-landed menu-readiness work or broad docs/cleanup churn.

## What changed

- Adds a single-instance launch guard so a later duplicate CodexBar process exits before creating a second menu bar instance.
- Makes Launch at Login disable semantics remove pending ServiceManagement registrations, including `.requiresApproval`, instead of treating them as no-ops.
- Serializes the keychain prompt alert on the main actor to avoid taking the prompt lock before a main-queue hop.
- Adds cold-start validation scripts that can prepare a one-shot post-reboot LaunchAgent, capture first-open menu artifacts, and validate the captured evidence.
- Hardens package/run scripts so local validation uses the expected packaged bundle and catches duplicate launched instances.
- Adds focused tests for the launch guard, login-item pending-registration behavior, and cold-start artifact checker.

## Why this is separate

The merged #1231 patch deliberately avoided duplicate-launch guards, launch validation harnesses, and keychain/login-item changes because those have different review risk than the menu refresh lifecycle itself. This PR keeps that leftover surface isolated so maintainers can review those runtime and operator-facing behaviors independently.

## Validation

- `bash -n Scripts/check_cold_start_validation_result.sh Scripts/prepare_next_boot_cold_start_validation.sh Scripts/validate_cold_start_menu.sh Scripts/compile_and_run.sh Scripts/package_app.sh`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `swift test --filter AppDelegateTests`
- `swift test --filter LaunchAtLoginManagerTests`
- `swift test --filter ColdStartValidationCheckerTests`
- `make check`

## Notes

This PR intentionally does not change the visible UI design. It is focused on launch behavior, ServiceManagement cleanup, keychain prompt safety, packaging/runtime validation, and proof tooling around the already-merged cold-start menu readiness work.
target.author: amrmohamad

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
